refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to operators and system managers.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement robust error correction and packet retransmission protocols.
DP2: Use adaptive bitrate and compression algorithms to optimize data flow.
DP3: Establish prioritized network traffic management for teleoperation data.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement robust error correction and packet retransmission protocols.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on communication protocol improvements that logically map to the FR without introducing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that error correction overhead may increase latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating adaptive bitrate (DP2) if packet loss remains high despite error correction.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication modules responsible for teleoperation control and data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics to the NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but teleoperation-specific logic is diffused.
Vehicle module mainly hardware-focused; minimal teleoperation communication logic.
Primary containment for teleoperation communication logic and protocols.
Safety alerts may interface but do not contain teleoperation communication logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes communication protocol logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for emergency communication.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review integration points with Safety Services to ensure emergency communication paths are robust.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best module for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network packet analysis.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical communication.
Monitor latency impact of error correction protocols.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the requirements with clear FR-DP-IM mapping, manageable risks, and feasible verification.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to operators and system managers.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module has sufficient capability to implement error correction.
open_risks: Latency impact of error correction on teleoperation responsiveness.
Integration with Safety Services for emergency communication.
evidence_needed: Packet loss measurements under operational conditions.
Latency and responsiveness metrics post-DP implementation.
